sub_pixel_system.notes: No subpixels in this game. Movement is pixel-based only.

special_mechanics.name: Flight (Jump Hold)
special_mechanics.description: Pit can hold jump button (A) during ascent to reduce gravity from 2 to 1 per frame, allowing variable jump height and sustained air control.

special_mechanics.name: Ice Physics
special_mechanics.description: On ice surfaces, maximum horizontal speed increases dramatically, requiring 65 frames to decelerate to a complete stop.

special_mechanics.name: In-Air Horizontal Speed
special_mechanics.description: When airborne, speed value 9 moves at 1 pixel/frame. Values 2-7 move 1 pixel every other frame when speed value is odd.

special_mechanics.name: Damage Knockback
special_mechanics.description: Taking damage from most enemies pushes Pit 5 pixels in one direction, equivalent to 5 frames of normal movement. Some enemies push right instead of left.

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical data extracted from ROM analysis and speedrun documentation. RAM addresses documented (001C for X speed, 001D for Y speed). Horizontal speed is 8-bit signed value; vertical speed uses non-linear conversion. No subpixel system present. Movement feels loose compared to later platformers, with 1-frame acceleration delay and variable gravity based on input.
sources_used: tasvideos - Horizontal speed analysis, vertical speed analysis, RAM addresses, physics conversion formulas
